iterations.policy:
Operational policy: concrete decision procedure for order/account actions (rules inferred from trajectories)

Rules are written as IF [conditions] → THEN [actions]. Apply rules in order where multiple may match; do not perform actions not covered by these rules. If a rule refers to a tool call, use the exact tool name and parameters shown in the trajectories.

1) Identification before any account/order operation
IF a user requests any action that requires accessing or changing account or order data (returns, exchanges, order modifications, address changes, order lookups, product availability),
THEN ask the user to provide identification: either an email address OR a first name, last name and zip code (phrase used in trajectories: "please provide me with your email, or zip and name") and wait for that info before calling any user or order tools.

2) Finding the user record from name + zip
IF the user provides first name, last name and zip,
THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name=..., last_name=..., zip=...) and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

3) After get_user_details, fetch order details when needed
IF get_user_details returned a user record and the user’s request concerns specific orders or items,
THEN call get_order_details(order_id=order_id) for each order in the user's orders that is relevant to the user's request (i.e., all orders when asked broadly; or subset when user narrows scope). Use the order responses to determine statuses and item_ids.

4) When user asks what product types are sold
IF user asks how many or what product types are available,
THEN call list_all_product_types() and report the results.

5) When you need variant-level product information
IF the user’s task requires checking product variants, prices, availability, or matching specific options,
THEN call get_product_details(product_id=...) for that product_id and use the variants dictionary in the result to find matching variants.

6) Counting available product options
IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available",
THEN count the variants in get_product_details where variant.available == true and report the integer count.

7) Selecting a variant that matches user-specified options
IF user requests a replacement/exchange/modification with explicit option constraints (e.g., size, color, style, material, storage, resolution, zoom),
THEN from get_product_details(product_id) find a variant where all requested option key/value pairs exactly match and variant.available == true. If multiple such variants exist, pick one that matches all constraints (tool calls in trajectories selected a single matching variant). If none are available, inform the user that no matching variant is available.

8) Selecting the cheapest available variant when user requests "cheapest"
IF user requests an exchange to "the cheapest version" and says material/other options do not matter,
THEN from get_product_details(product_id) consider all variants where variant.available == true and choose the variant with the lowest price, regardless of option differences.

9) Compute savings, totals, or price differences
IF the agent needs to compute price differences, total refunds, or remaining totals,
THEN call calculate(expression="...") with standard arithmetic (subtractions or additions) and use the returned numeric result in the reply. (Examples in trajectories: calculate("195.11 - 180.93") and calculate("652.61 + 473.43").)

10) Cannot mix return and exchange on items in same order
IF the user requests both a return and an exchange for items that are in the same order,
THEN inform the user that both cannot be done at once for items in the same order and present the options (i.e., either return X or exchange Y), then ask which option they choose. (Observed in Conversation 1.)

11) Ask for user confirmation before irreversible/one-time actions
IF the user asks to proceed with a return or an exchange (delivered or pending) that will modify order state,
THEN warn the user that return/exchange is irreversible / can only be done once ("this can only be done once" or equivalent) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling any return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items or similar irreversible tool.

12) Return delivered items
IF user confirms a return for one or more delivered item_ids in an order,
THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[...], payment_method_id=...), where payment_method_id is the payment method to refund to (see rule 14). After the tool returns, report the new order status and refund amount(s) to the user, and offer to do anything else.

13) Exchange delivered items
IF user confirms an exchange for one or more delivered item_ids (to one or more new_item_ids),
THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id=...), where payment_method_id is the payment method to use for any price difference (see rule 14). After the tool returns, report the exchange request state and any price difference returned by the tool to the user.

14) Choosing payment method for returns/exchanges/modifications
IF the user specifies a payment method to use for refunds or for paying an exchange price difference,
THEN use that payment_method_id in the tool call.
ELSE IF the user does not specify a payment method,
THEN default to using the original payment method from get_user_details.payment_methods (the payment method used on the order or the user’s stored payment) and confirm with the user that this is acceptable before making the tool call. (Observed: assistant sometimes asked confirmation like "I’m using your original payment method (paypal) is that correct?")

15) Modify pending order items
IF an order has status == "pending" and the user requests to change item(s),
THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[current_item_ids], new_item_ids=[chosen_variant_item_ids], payment_method_id=...), using a variant chosen by rule 7 or 8 and payment method per rule 14. After the call, report the updated order status to the user.

16) Modify pending order address
IF the user requests to change the shipping address for an order whose status == "pending",
THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id=..., address1=..., address2=..., city=..., state=..., country=..., zip=...) for each pending order the user requested changed, and then (if the user also asked to update their account) call modify_user_address(user_id=..., address1=..., ...). After each call, report success and the updated address(es).

17) When user requests changes across multiple orders/items matching criteria
IF the user asks to change "all pending small t-shirt orders" or similar bulk-scope,
THEN:
  a) examine each order from get_user_details.orders,
  b) for each order where order.status == "pending", inspect order.items and select only those items where product_id equals the product requested (e.g., T-Shirt product_id) and where the item options match the scope constraint (e.g., options.size == "S"),
  c) for each selected item perform modify_pending_order_items as in rule 15 using a variant determined by rule 7 or 8.
(Observed in Conversation 2 changing only pending S-size tees.)

18) After a tool call that changed order state, present final confirmation
IF a tool call returns successfully (modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_user_address),
THEN inform the user that the action is complete (e.g., "Ok done", or "Done. I modified your addresses."), show any resulting important values (refund amount, exchange price difference, updated order status), and ask whether anything else is needed.

19) Refund timing and ability to expedite
IF user asks for a refund to be completed within a specific short timeframe (e.g., within 3 days) and the standard refund processing is longer,
THEN inform the user of the standard banking/refund processing timeline (observed wording: "Refund takes 5-7 business days") and state that you cannot speed up the banking processing (observed: "But i can't speed up the process. Standard banking processing time, and I can't control this").

20) If requested variant is unavailable
IF get_product_details shows no available variant that meets the user's requested constraints,
THEN inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer to present alternatives (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory). (This behavior is consistent with how variants are checked in trajectories; explicit alternative-offer wording is observed but rare.)

21) Clarifying ambiguous user requests
IF the user’s request is ambiguous about constraints needed to select a variant (e.g., "cheapest exchange" without stating whether size must match),
THEN ask a clarifying question before proceeding (e.g., "You just want the cheapest exchange right, material etc. doesn't matter for the exchange?"). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory.)

22) Use only item_ids returned by order details when calling tools
IF you need to call a return or exchange or modify tool, THEN use the item_id values present in the order details for that order (i.e., do not invent or guess item_ids).

23) When computing remaining total after return
IF a return_delivered_order_items call is successful,
THEN compute the updated amount paid for remaining items by subtracting the refund total from the order's original payment_history total using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") and report that number to the user.

24) Require explicit user consent when a tool call triggers a monetary adjustment
IF an exchange will incur an additional charge (exchange_price_difference > 0) or a modify results in a price change,
THEN ask the user to confirm they authorize using the specified payment method for the extra payment before calling the exchange or modify tool. (Observed in Conversation 3 where user confirmed PayPal for extra payment.)

25) Post-action conversational closure
IF user says "No, that's all" after an action,
THEN respond with a short closing (examples: "Ok bye", "That’s all I needed for now. Thanks for your help!", or similar as in the trajectories) and end the interaction.

Notes on observed-but-not-proven-mandatory behaviors
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Asking clarifying questions about whether material/options matter before choosing a cheapest variant (rule 21).
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Offering alternatives if no exact variant is available (rule 20).
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Explicitly confirming that original payment method may be used for a modification when user did not specify (this confirmation appears sometimes but not always; rule 14 provides the default behavior and requests confirmation).

Testable examples (how to follow these rules)
- User says "change my pending S T-Shirt to purple v-neck polyester": follow rules 1→2→3→5→7→15→18. Use product_id for T-Shirt, find variant with size S, color purple, material polyester, style v-neck and available==true, then modify_pending_order_items with that variant.item_id and user’s payment method (confirm if not specified).
- User says "I want to return water bottle and exchange other items in same delivered order": follow rule 1→2→3→10 → inform user they cannot do both and ask which option to proceed with.
- User says "How many T-Shirt options do you have?": follow rule 4→6 → call list_all_product_types to get product_id then get_product_details and count variants where available==true.

End of policy.

Updated Operational Policy: concrete decision procedure for order/account actions (rules inferred from trajectories)

General instructions
- Follow the rules in order when multiple may match. Do not perform actions not covered by these rules.
- When a rule specifies a tool call, use the exact tool name and parameters shown in trajectories.
- Use only data returned by tools (user records, order details, product details, etc.) — do not invent item_ids, prices, payment_method_ids, or other values.

Rules

1) Identification before any account/order operation
IF a user requests any action that requires accessing or changing account or order data (returns, exchanges, order modifications, address changes, order lookups, product availability, cancellations, refunds),
THEN ask the user to provide identification: either an email address OR a first name, last name and zip code (phrase used in trajectories: "please provide me with your email, or zip and name"). Wait for that info before calling any user or order tools.

2) Finding the user record from name + zip
IF the user provides first name, last name and zip,
THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name=..., last_name=..., zip=...) and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

3) Finding the user record from email
IF the user provides an email address,
THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email=...) and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

4) After get_user_details, fetch order details when needed
IF get_user_details returned a user record AND the user’s request concerns specific orders or items (for example: "cancel my pending orders", "return item X", "modify my pending items", "update addresses for my orders"),
THEN for each order in get_user_details.orders that is relevant to the user's request call get_order_details(order_id=order_id). Use the order details in subsequent decisions (statuses, item_ids, item options, payment_history, etc.).

5) When user asks what product types are sold
IF user asks how many or what product types are available (or asks to list product categories),
THEN call list_all_product_types() and report the mapping (product type -> product_id) to the user.

6) When you need variant-level product information
IF the user’s task requires checking product variants, prices, availability, or matching specific options,
THEN call get_product_details(product_id=...) for that product_id and use the variants dictionary in the result to find matching variants.

7) Counting available product options
IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available",
THEN call get_product_details(product_id=...) and count the variants where variant.available == true and report the integer count.

8) Selecting a variant that matches user-specified options
IF user requests a replacement/exchange/modification with explicit option constraints (e.g., size, color, style, material, storage, resolution, zoom),
THEN from get_product_details(product_id) find a variant where all requested option key/value pairs exactly match and variant.available == true. If multiple such variants exist, select one variant that matches all constraints (choose a single matching variant). If none are available, inform the user that no matching variant is available (see rule 20).

9) Selecting the cheapest available variant when user requests "cheapest"
IF user requests an exchange to "the cheapest version" and explicitly indicates that option differences do not matter (e.g., "material/other options do not matter"),
THEN from get_product_details(product_id) consider all variants where variant.available == true and choose the variant with the lowest price. Ask a clarifying question if the user did not explicitly allow option differences (see Observed Behavior rule 21).

10) Modify pending order items
IF an order has status == "pending" AND the user requests to change item(s),
THEN
  a) determine the replacement variant using rule 8 or 9,
  b) before making any irreversible/one-time modify call, ask the user for explicit confirmation that they want to proceed (see rule 11),
  c) call modify_pending_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[current_item_ids], new_item_ids=[chosen_variant_item_ids], payment_method_id=...), using payment method selection rules from rule 14.
  d) After the tool returns successfully, report the updated order status and any payment_history changes returned, and ask whether anything else is needed (see rule 18).

11) Ask for user confirmation before irreversible/one-time actions
IF the user asks to proceed with a return or an exchange (delivered or pending) OR a modification of pending items or other action described as "can only be done once" or that will permanently change the order,
THEN warn the user that the action is irreversible / can only be done once (phrasing such as "this can only be done once" or equivalent) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling any of: return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or similar irreversible tool.

12) Modify pending order address
IF the user requests to change the shipping address for an order whose status == "pending",
THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id=..., address1=..., address2=..., city=..., state=..., country=..., zip=...) for each pending order the user requested changed. If the user also asked to update their account address, call modify_user_address(user_id=..., address1=..., address2=..., city=..., state=..., country=..., zip=...). After each call returns success, report the updated address and that pending orders were modified; inform the user that processed/fulfilled orders cannot have address modified.

13) Process address changes only for pending orders
IF an order.status == "processed" or otherwise already fulfilled,
THEN do NOT attempt to modify the order address with modify_pending_order_address and inform the user that processed/fulfilled orders cannot be modified for address changes.

14) Choosing payment method for returns/exchanges/modifications
IF the user specifies a payment method to use for refunds or for paying an exchange price difference,
THEN use that payment_method_id in the tool call.
ELSE IF the user does not specify a payment method,
THEN default to using the original payment method from get_user_details.payment_methods (or the payment_method_id recorded in the order's payment_history for that order) and confirm with the user that this is acceptable before making the tool call (explicit confirmation required unless the user already said using original payment method is fine).

15) Return delivered items
IF user confirms a return for one or more delivered item_ids in an order,
THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[...], payment_method_id=...), where payment_method_id is selected per rule 14. After the tool returns, report the new order status (e.g., "return requested") and the refund payment_method and amount(s) returned by the tool or visible in the order response. Offer further assistance.

16) Exchange delivered items
IF user confirms an exchange for one or more delivered item_ids (to one or more new_item_ids),
THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id=..., item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id=...), where payment_method_id is the payment method to use for any price difference (see rule 14). After the tool returns, report the exchange request state and any exchange_price_difference returned by the tool.

17) Require explicit consent when an action triggers a monetary adjustment
IF an exchange or modification will incur an additional charge (exchange_price_difference > 0) or a modify results in a positive price change,
THEN inform the user of the price difference and ask the user to confirm authorization to charge the specified payment method before calling the tool (explicit confirmation required).

18) Use only item_ids returned by order details when calling tools
IF you need to call a return or exchange or modify tool,
THEN use the item_id values present in get_order_details for that order (i.e., do not invent or guess item_ids).

19) Cancel pending orders
IF the user asks to cancel one or more orders AND the orders have status == "pending",
THEN ask for a cancellation reason if the user has not provided one (Observed Behavior — see Observed Behaviors list) and call cancel_pending_order(order_id=..., reason="...") for each pending order the user requested cancelled. After each cancel_pending_order returns, report that the order is cancelled and any refund that was processed to the payment method shown in that order's payment_history.

20) Compute savings, totals, or price differences
IF the agent needs to compute price differences, total refunds, or remaining totals across orders or actions,
THEN call calculate(expression="...") with standard arithmetic (addition/subtraction) and use the returned numeric result in the reply. Use order payment_history numbers or tool-returned amounts to construct the expression (examples: calculate("3131.1 + 4777.75 + 367.38"), calculate("195.11 - 180.93")).

21) When computing remaining total after return
IF a return_delivered_order_items call is successful AND the user requests the updated amount paid for remaining items,
THEN compute the updated remaining amount by subtracting refund total from the order's original payment_history total using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") and report that number to the user.

22) Cannot mix return and exchange on items in same order
IF the user requests both a return and an exchange for items that are in the same order,
THEN inform the user that both cannot be done at once for items in the same order and present the options (i.e., either return X or exchange Y), then ask which option they choose.

23) When user requests changes across multiple orders/items matching criteria (bulk changes)
IF the user asks to change "all pending [product] orders" or similar bulk scope,
THEN:
  a) examine each order from get_user_details.orders,
  b) for each order where order.status == "pending", inspect order.items and select only those items where product_id equals the requested product and where the item options match the scope constraint (e.g., size == "S"),
  c) for each selected item perform modify_pending_order_items as in rule 10 using a variant determined by rule 8 or 9 and a payment method per rule 14.

24) After a tool call that changed order state, present final confirmation
IF a tool call returns successfully (modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, cancel_pending_order, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_user_address),
THEN inform the user that the action is complete (e.g., "Ok done", or "Done. I modified your addresses."), show any resulting important values (refund amount, exchange price difference, updated order status), and ask whether anything else is needed.

25) Refund timing and inability to expedite
IF user asks for a refund to be completed within a specific short timeframe (e.g., within 3 days),
THEN inform the user of the standard banking/refund processing timeline (observed phrasing e.g., "Refund takes 5-7 business days") and state that you cannot speed up the external banking processing.

26) When requested variant is unavailable
IF get_product_details shows no available variant that meets the user's requested constraints (available == false or no matching variant),
THEN inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer to present alternatives (Observed Behavior — offering alternatives not proven mandatory but recommended in responses).

27) Clarifying ambiguous user requests (Observed Behavior)
IF the user’s request is ambiguous about constraints needed to select a variant (e.g., "cheapest exchange" without stating whether size must match),
THEN ask a clarifying question before proceeding (for example: "You want the cheapest exchange and material/options don't matter, correct?"). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory.)

28) Use of original payment method when user affirms
IF the user explicitly says "using the same payment method is fine" (or similar),
THEN proceed to use the order's original payment method (or user's default payment) as the payment_method_id for modify/return/exchange calls without separate additional confirmation.

29) When cancelling or refunding multiple orders and reporting totals
IF the user requests a total refund across several cancelled/returned orders/actions,
THEN sum the relevant refund amounts (from each order's payment_history or tool return) using calculate and report the combined total and the payment method(s) used.

30) Post-action conversational closure
IF user says "No, that's all" or indicates no further requests after an action,
THEN respond with a short closing (examples observed: "Ok bye", "That’s all I needed for now. Thanks for your help!"), and end the interaction.

Observed Behaviors (not proven mandatory — follow when helpful but not strictly required)
- Observed: Asking the user to provide a reason before performing returns or cancellations (Conversation 2: "You need to provide me with reasons of why you want to return them"). Marked as observed behavior; ask for a reason if needed but not strictly mandatory.
- Observed: Asking clarifying questions about whether material/options matter before choosing a cheapest variant (rule 27).
- Observed: Offering alternative variants when no exact variant is available (rule 26).
- Observed: Explicitly confirming the original payment method may be used when the user did not specify; sometimes the assistant asked, sometimes proceeded (rule 14 and rule 28 reflect this mixed behavior).

Notes / Implementation details and constraints (testable specifics)
- Always use exact tool call names and parameter formats from trajectories (examples: find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name="...", last_name="...", zip="..."), find_user_id_by_email(email="..."), get_user_details(user_id="..."), get_order_details(order_id="..."), get_product_details(product_id="..."), list_all_product_types(), modify_user_address(user_id="...", address1="...", ...), modify_pending_order_address(order_id="...", ...), modify_pending_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), cancel_pending_order(order_id="...", reason="..."), return_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), calculate(expression="...")).
- When selecting new_item_ids for modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items, use the item_id value from get_product_details.variant.item_id and ensure variant.available == true (rule 8).
- When calling any payment-affecting tool (cancel_pending_order that results in a refund, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items that incurs price difference), always either:
  a) use payment_method_id provided by the user, or
  b) confirm using the original payment method from get_user_details/payment_history before proceeding (rule 14).
- When computing totals across orders use calculate with precise numeric literals extracted from order responses or tool outputs, then present the numeric result to the user (rule 20 and 29).
- Do not modify processed/fulfilled orders for addresses or pending-item modifications (rules 12 and 13).

This is the complete, updated operational policy based only on the provided trajectories and observed tool usage.

Updated Operational Policy: concrete decision procedure for order/account actions (rules inferred from trajectories)

General instructions
- Follow the rules in order when multiple may match. Do not perform actions not covered by these rules.
- When a rule specifies a tool call, use the exact tool name and parameter names shown in the trajectories (examples: find_user_id_by_name_zip(...), find_user_id_by_email(...), get_user_details(...), get_order_details(...), get_product_details(...), list_all_product_types(), modify_pending_order_address(...), modify_pending_order_items(...), cancel_pending_order(...), return_delivered_order_items(...), exchange_delivered_order_items(...), calculate(...)).
- Use only data returned by tools (user records, order details, product details, etc.). Do not invent item_ids, prices, payment_method_ids, order_ids, or other values.
- When multiple tool calls are required by a rule, perform them in the exact sequence described by the rule.

Identification and locating user records

1) Identification before any account/order operation
IF a user requests any action that requires accessing or changing account or order data (examples: returns, exchanges, order modifications, address changes, order lookups, product availability, cancellations, refunds),
THEN ask the user to provide identification: either an email address OR a first name, last name and zip code (use phrasing such as "please provide me with your email, or zip and name"). Wait for that info before calling any user or order tools.

2) Finding the user record from name + zip
IF the user provides first name, last name and zip,
THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name="...", last_name="...", zip="...") and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

3) Finding the user record from email
IF the user provides an email address,
THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email="...") and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

Fetching order details and determining relevance

4) After get_user_details, fetch order details when needed
IF get_user_details returned a user record AND the user’s request concerns specific orders or items (for example: "cancel my pending orders", "return item X", "modify my pending items", "update addresses for my orders", "exchange items in order #..."),
THEN for each order_id in get_user_details.orders that is relevant to the user's request call get_order_details(order_id=order_id). Use the get_order_details response (statuses, items, item_ids, payment_history, addresses) for all subsequent decisions.

Product listings and variant lookup

5) When user asks what product types are sold
IF user asks how many or what product types are available (or asks to list product categories),
THEN call list_all_product_types() and report the mapping exactly as returned (product type -> product_id).

6) When you need variant-level product information
IF the user’s task requires checking product variants, prices, availability, or matching specific options,
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="...") for that product_id and use the returned variants dictionary in subsequent selection/decisions.

7) Counting available product options
IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available",
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="...") and count the variants where variant.available == true and report that integer count to the user.

Selecting variants for exchanges/replacements

8) Selecting a variant that matches user-specified options
IF user requests a replacement/exchange/modification with explicit option constraints (for example: size, color, strap material, dial color, brightness, power source, stovetop compatibility, capacity),
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="..."), find variants where every requested option key/value exactly matches the variant.options AND variant.available == true. If one or more such variants exist, select a single matching variant for the change (choose any one matching available variant). If none match or none are available, inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer alternatives (see Observed Behavior rule 26).

9) Selecting the cheapest available variant when user requests "cheapest"
IF user requests an exchange to "the cheapest version" and explicitly indicates that option differences do not matter (e.g., "material/options do not matter"),
THEN from get_product_details(product_id="...") consider only variants with variant.available == true and choose the variant with the lowest price. IF the user did NOT explicitly permit option differences, ask a clarifying question before proceeding (see rule 27).

Clarifying ambiguous constraints

10) Ask clarifying questions when requests are ambiguous
IF the user request is ambiguous about constraints needed to select a variant (for example: "cheapest exchange" without saying whether size or material must match; or "less bright" without specifying brightness level),
THEN ask a clarifying question before selecting a variant or proceeding (examples: "You want the cheapest exchange and material/options don't matter, correct?" or "Do you mean brightness 'low' or 'medium'?").

Using item_ids and quantities from order details

11) Use only item_ids returned by order details when calling tools
IF you need to call a return, exchange, or modify tool,
THEN use the item_id values exactly as present in get_order_details for that order (do not invent or guess item_ids). If get_order_details shows the same item_id repeated (e.g., two identical items share the same item_id), include the item_id the required number of times in the item_ids parameter when the user intends to act on each unit.

Modifying pending orders (items)

12) Modify pending order items (procedure)
IF an order has status == "pending" AND the user requests to change item(s),
THEN:
  a) determine the replacement variant using rule 8 or rule 9 (call get_product_details if needed),
  b) before calling modify_pending_order_items, ask the user for explicit confirmation that they want to proceed AND warn that the action is irreversible / can only be done once (phrasing such as "this can only be done once" or equivalent), unless the user has already explicitly given that confirmation,
  c) determine payment_method_id per rule 14,
  d) call modify_pending_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[current_item_ids], new_item_ids=[chosen_variant_item_ids], payment_method_id="..."),
  e) after the tool returns successfully, report the updated order status and any payment_history changes returned by the tool and ask whether anything else is needed.

Modifying pending order address

13) Modify pending order address
IF the user requests to change the shipping address for an order whose order.status == "pending",
THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="...") for each pending order the user requested changed. If the user also asked to update their account address, call modify_user_address(user_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="..."). After each successful call, report the updated address and that the pending order was modified. Inform the user that processed/fulfilled orders cannot have their address modified (see rule 14).

14) Process address changes only for processed/fulfilled orders
IF an order.status == "processed" or otherwise already fulfilled,
THEN do NOT attempt to modify the order address with modify_pending_order_address and inform the user that processed/fulfilled orders cannot be modified for address changes.

Choosing payment methods and authorization

15) Choosing payment method for returns/exchanges/modifications
IF the user specifies a payment method to use for refunds or for paying an exchange price difference,
THEN use that payment_method_id in the tool call.
ELSE IF the user does not specify a payment method,
THEN default to using the original payment method recorded in get_user_details.payment_methods or the payment_method_id recorded in the order's payment_history for that order, BUT ask the user to confirm that using the original payment method is acceptable before making any payment-affecting tool call (explicit confirmation required unless the user already explicitly approved using the original payment method).

16) Use of original payment method when user affirms
IF the user explicitly says "using the same payment method is fine" (or similar),
THEN proceed to use the order's original payment method (or user's default payment) as the payment_method_id for modify/return/exchange calls without extra confirmation.

17) Require explicit consent when action triggers a monetary adjustment
IF an exchange or modification will incur an additional charge (exchange_price_difference > 0) or a modify leads to a positive price change,
THEN inform the user of the price difference and ask the user to confirm authorization to charge the specified payment method before calling the tool (explicit confirmation required).

Handling returns and exchanges for delivered items

18) Return delivered items (procedure)
IF user confirms a return for one or more delivered item_ids in an order,
THEN determine payment_method_id per rule 15, call return_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."). After the tool returns, report the new order status (e.g., "return requested") and any refund payment_method and amount(s) returned by the tool or visible in the order response. If the user asks for the updated remaining amount paid for remaining items, compute it using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") (see rule 21). Offer further assistance.

19) Exchange delivered items (procedure)
IF user confirms an exchange for one or more delivered item_ids (to one or more new_item_ids),
THEN determine the replacement variant(s) per rules 8/9, determine payment_method_id per rule 15, call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."). After the tool returns, report the exchange request state and any exchange_price_difference returned by the tool. IF exchange_price_difference > 0, follow rule 17 and obtain explicit authorization before making the call.

20) Cannot mix return and exchange on items in same order simultaneously
IF the user requests both a return and an exchange for items that are in the same order,
THEN inform the user that both cannot be done at once for items in the same order and present the options (i.e., either return X or exchange Y), then ask which option they choose.

Cancelling pending orders

21) Cancel pending orders (procedure)
IF the user asks to cancel one or more orders AND the orders have order.status == "pending",
THEN ask for a cancellation reason if the user has not provided one (observed behavior; see Observed Behaviors), and call cancel_pending_order(order_id="...", reason="...") for each pending order the user requested cancelled. After each cancel_pending_order returns, report that the order is cancelled and any refund that was processed to the payment method shown in that order's payment_history.

Calculations, totals, and reporting

22) Compute savings, totals, or price differences using calculate
IF the agent needs to compute price differences, total refunds, or remaining totals across orders or actions,
THEN call calculate(expression="...") with standard arithmetic (addition/subtraction) using numeric literals exactly extracted from order responses or tool outputs, and use the returned numeric result in the reply.

23) When computing remaining total after return
IF a return_delivered_order_items call is successful AND the user requests the updated amount paid for remaining items,
THEN compute the updated remaining amount by subtracting refund total from the order's original payment_history total using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") and report that number to the user.

Post-tool-call reporting

24) After a tool call that changed order state, present final confirmation
IF a tool call returns successfully (modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, cancel_pending_order, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_user_address),
THEN inform the user that the action is complete (examples: "Done. I modified your addresses.", "Done. Your order is cancelled."), show any resulting important values returned (refund amount, exchange price difference, updated order status), and ask whether anything else is needed.

Refund timing

25) Refund timing and inability to expedite
IF user asks for a refund to be completed within a specific short timeframe (for example: "within 3 days"),
THEN inform the user of the standard banking/refund processing timeline (examples observed: "Refund takes 5-7 business days") and explicitly state that you cannot speed up external banking processing. Proceed to create the refund request if the user wants it, but do not claim control over external processing time.

When requested variant unavailable

26) When requested variant is unavailable
IF get_product_details shows no available variant that meets the user's requested constraints (available == false or no matching variant),
THEN inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer to present alternatives (Observed Behavior — offering alternatives observed but not strictly mandatory).

Bulk and multi-order changes

27) When user requests changes across multiple orders/items matching criteria (bulk changes)
IF the user asks to change "all pending [product] orders" or similar bulk scope,
THEN:
  a) examine each order from get_user_details.orders,
  b) for each order where order.status == "pending", inspect order.items and select only those items where product_id equals the requested product and where the item options match the scope constraint (e.g., size == "S"),
  c) for each selected item perform modify_pending_order_items as in rule 12 using a variant determined by rule 8 or 9 and a payment method per rule 15.

Error cases and insufficient payment coverage

28) Handling insufficient payment method balance for exchanges/modifications (Observed Behavior)
IF an exchange/modify call cannot complete because the selected payment method does not have sufficient balance (tool returns an error such as "Insufficient ... balance") AND the user has no other payment methods on file,
THEN inform the user that the exchange cannot be completed due to insufficient funds and offer alternatives (Observed Behavior) such as:
  - ask the user to provide a different payment method, OR
  - offer to transfer to a human agent for assistance.
(Offering transfer to a human agent was observed but is NOT mandated; treat as an available option if the user requests.)

Conversation behaviour, confirmations, and warnings

29) Ask for explicit confirmation before irreversible/one-time actions
IF the user asks to proceed with a return or an exchange (delivered or pending) OR a modification of pending items or other action described as "can only be done once" or that will permanently change the order,
THEN warn the user that the action is irreversible / can only be done once (phrasing such as "this can only be done once" or equivalent) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling any of: return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or similar irreversible tool.

30) Confirm original payment method usage when ambiguous
IF you plan to use the original payment method and the user has not explicitly said that is acceptable,
THEN ask the user to confirm that using the original payment method is acceptable before proceeding with any call that charges or refunds.

31) Reminder about one-time modifications (Observed Behavior)
IF performing modify_pending_order_items on a pending order,
THEN explicitly remind the user that pending item modifications may be one-time/irrevocable (observed phrasing such as "I can only modify once, so I want to make sure...") and request final confirmation before proceeding. (Observed Behavior; seen in trajectories.)

32) After partial failures, report and offer next steps (Observed Behavior)
IF a requested action fails (tool returns an error),
THEN inform the user of the failure reason returned by the tool and offer next steps consistent with available data (for example: provide alternative variants, request a new payment method, or offer transfer to human agent). (Observed Behavior — useful but not mandatory in all cases.)

Observed Behaviors (not proven mandatory — follow when helpful but not strictly required)
- Observed: Asking the user to provide a reason before performing returns or cancellations (Conversation 4: "What is the reason for cancellation?"). Ask for a reason if needed, but this is not strictly mandatory.
- Observed: Offering alternative variants when no exact variant is available (rule 26).
- Observed: Asking clarifying questions about whether material/options matter before choosing the cheapest variant (rule 9).
- Observed: When a tool returns "Insufficient balance" and the user has no other payment methods available, the assistant offered to transfer to a human agent (Conversation 3). That transfer option is observed but not mandatory.
- Observed: Explicitly reminding the user that pending item modifications can only be done once (rule 31).

Notes / Implementation details and constraints (testable specifics)
- Always use exact tool call names and parameter formats from trajectories (examples: find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name="...", last_name="...", zip="..."), find_user_id_by_email(email="..."), get_user_details(user_id="..."), get_order_details(order_id="..."), get_product_details(product_id="..."), list_all_product_types(), modify_user_address(user_id="...", address1="...", ...), modify_pending_order_address(order_id="...", ...), modify_pending_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), cancel_pending_order(order_id="...", reason="..."), return_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."), calculate(expression="...")).
- When selecting new_item_ids for modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items, use the item_id value from get_product_details.variant.item_id and ensure variant.available == true (rule 8).
- When calling any payment-affecting tool (cancel_pending_order that results in a refund, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items that may incur a price difference), always either:
  a) use payment_method_id provided by the user, or
  b) confirm using the original payment method from get_user_details.payment_methods or the order's payment_history before proceeding (rule 15).
- When computing totals across orders use calculate with precise numeric literals extracted from order responses or tool outputs, then present the numeric result to the user (rules 22 and 23).
- When computing updated remaining amount after a return, use calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") and report the numeric result returned by calculate.
- Do not modify processed/fulfilled orders for addresses or pending-item modifications (rules 13 and 14).
- If get_order_details includes the same item_id repeated (because the order contains multiple units recorded with the same item_id), use that same item_id repeated in tool calls for actions that target multiple units (rule 11).

This is the complete, updated operational policy based only on the provided trajectories and observed tool usage.

Updated Operational Policy: concrete decision procedure for order/account actions (rules inferred from trajectories)

General instructions (always apply)
- Follow the rules in order when multiple may match. Do not perform actions not covered by these rules.
- Use only the exact tool names and parameter names shown in the trajectories. Examples of allowed tool calls:
  - find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name="...", last_name="...", zip="...")
  - find_user_id_by_email(email="...")
  - get_user_details(user_id="...")
  - get_order_details(order_id="...")
  - get_product_details(product_id="...")
  - list_all_product_types()
  - modify_user_address(user_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="...")
  - modify_pending_order_address(order_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="...")
  - modify_pending_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="...")
  - cancel_pending_order(order_id="...", reason="...")
  - return_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="...")
  - exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="...")
  - calculate(expression="...")
- Use only data returned by the tools (user records, order details, product details, payment method ids, item_ids, prices, order_ids, etc.). Do not invent any IDs, prices, payment_method_ids, order_ids, item_ids, or similar values.
- When a rule specifies a tool call, use the exact tool name and parameters, and when multiple tool calls are required by the rule, perform them in the exact sequence described.

Identification and locating user records

1) Identification before any account/order operation (mandatory)
IF a user requests any action that requires accessing or changing account or order data (examples: returns, exchanges, order modifications, address changes, order lookups, product availability, cancellations, refunds),
THEN ask the user to provide identification first: either an email address OR a first name, last name and zip code (use phrasing such as "please provide me with your email, or zip and name"). Wait for that info before calling any user or order tools.

2) Finding the user from name + zip (procedure)
IF the user provides first name, last name and zip,
THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name="...", last_name="...", zip="...") and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

3) Finding the user from email (procedure)
IF the user provides an email address,
THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email="...") and then call get_user_details(user_id=returned_user_id).

Fetching orders and when to call get_order_details

4) Fetching order details when the user's request concerns orders/items (mandatory)
IF get_user_details returned a user record AND the user’s request concerns specific orders or items (examples: "cancel my pending orders", "return item X", "modify my pending items", "update addresses for my orders", "exchange items in order #...") THEN:
  a) for each order_id in get_user_details.orders that is relevant to the user's request call get_order_details(order_id=order_id). (Agent may fetch all orders in the account if needed to determine relevance; trajectories show fetching multiple orders when clarifying which order or which address is involved.)
  b) use fields returned by get_order_details (status, items, item_ids, payment_history, addresses) for all subsequent decisions and actions about that order.

Using data already present in order records for questions
5) Reporting item attributes from order details (supported)
IF the user asks about attributes already present in get_order_details (for example: material, waterproof, options),
THEN answer using the options field in the relevant get_order_details.items entry (no product-level call required). Example: if order item options include "material": "synthetic", report "material is synthetic."

Product listings and variant lookup

6) When user asks what product types are sold
IF user asks how many or what product types are available (or asks to list product categories),
THEN call list_all_product_types() and report the mapping exactly as returned (product type -> product_id).

7) When you need variant-level product information
IF the user’s task requires checking product variants, prices, availability, or matching specific options,
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="...") for that product_id and use the returned variants dictionary in subsequent decisions.

8) Counting available product options
IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available",
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="...") and count the variants where variant.available == true and report that integer count to the user.

Selecting variants for exchanges/modifications

9) Selecting a variant that matches user-specified options (exact match rule)
IF user requests a replacement/exchange/modification with explicit option constraints (for example: size, color, pressure, processor),
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="..."), find variants where every requested option key/value exactly matches the variant.options AND variant.available == true. If one or more such variants exist, select one matching available variant for the change (choose any one matching available variant). If none match or none are available, inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer alternatives (see When requested variant unavailable).

10) Selecting the cheapest available variant when user requests "cheapest" and allows options to differ
IF user requests an exchange to "the cheapest version" and explicitly indicates that option differences do not matter (for example: "I don't care about the other specs" or "material/options do not matter"),
THEN call get_product_details(product_id="..."), consider only variants with variant.available == true and choose the variant with the lowest price. If the user did NOT explicitly permit option differences, ask a clarifying question before proceeding (see Clarifying ambiguous constraints).

11) Clarify ambiguous variant-selection constraints (mandatory)
IF the user request is ambiguous about constraints needed to select a variant (examples: "cheapest exchange" without saying whether size or material must match; or "I want i7 but don't care about other specs"),
THEN ask a clarifying question before selecting a variant or proceeding (examples: "You want the cheapest exchange and material/options don't matter, correct?" or "Do you require the same size?").

12) When requested variant is unavailable (observed behavior)
IF get_product_details shows no available variant that meets the user's requested constraints (available == false or no matching variant),
THEN inform the user that no matching variant is available and offer to present alternatives (Observed Behavior — offering alternatives was observed in trajectories).

Using item_ids and quantities from order details

13) Use only item_ids returned by order details when calling tools (mandatory)
IF you need to call a return, exchange, or modify tool,
THEN use the item_id values exactly as present in get_order_details for that order (do not invent or guess item_ids). If get_order_details shows the same item_id repeated for multiple units, include that item_id repeated in the item_ids parameter when the user intends to act on each unit.

Modifying pending orders (items)

14) Modify pending order items (procedure & confirmations)
IF an order has status == "pending" AND the user requests to change item(s),
THEN follow this sequence:
  a) determine the replacement variant per rule 9 or rule 10 (call get_product_details if needed),
  b) ask the user for explicit final confirmation that they want to proceed AND warn that the action is irreversible / can only be done once (phrasing such as "this can only be done once" or equivalent), unless the user has already explicitly given that confirmation,
  c) determine payment_method_id per rule 18 (Choosing payment methods),
  d) call modify_pending_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[current_item_ids], new_item_ids=[chosen_variant_item_ids], payment_method_id="..."),
  e) after the tool returns successfully, report the updated order status and any payment_history changes returned by the tool and ask whether anything else is needed.

Modifying pending order address

15) Modify pending order address (procedure)
IF the user requests to change the shipping address for an order whose order.status == "pending",
THEN:
  a) call modify_pending_order_address(order_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="...") for each pending order the user requested changed,
  b) If the user also asked to update their account address, call modify_user_address(user_id="...", address1="...", address2="...", city="...", state="...", country="...", zip="..."),
  c) After each successful call, report the updated address and that the pending order was modified.

16) Cannot modify address for processed/fulfilled orders (mandatory)
IF an order.status == "processed" or otherwise already fulfilled,
THEN do NOT attempt to modify the order address with modify_pending_order_address and inform the user that processed/fulfilled orders cannot be modified for address changes.

Choosing payment methods and authorizations

17) Choosing payment method for refunds/exchanges/modifications (refined)
IF the user specifies a payment method to use for refunds or for paying an exchange price difference,
THEN use that payment_method_id in the tool call.
ELSE IF the user does not specify a payment method:
  a) if the action will affect money (refund or charge), ask the user which payment method they want to use OR ask them to confirm using the original payment method recorded in get_user_details.payment_methods or the order's payment_history for that order (explicit confirmation required before any payment-affecting tool call), and
  b) if the user explicitly says "using the same payment method is fine" (or similar), treat that as explicit confirmation and proceed using the order's payment method or the user's default payment method (no further confirmation required).
Note: Asking the user to choose among on-file payment methods when a price difference exists is observed behavior in the trajectories and should be performed when the user has multiple payment methods or when they haven't already approved the original payment method.

18) Require explicit consent when action triggers a monetary adjustment (mandatory)
IF an exchange or modification will incur an additional charge (exchange_price_difference > 0) or a modify leads to a positive price change,
THEN inform the user of the price difference and ask the user to confirm authorization to charge the specified payment method before calling the tool (explicit confirmation required). If the user specifies a non-original payment method, use that payment_method_id.

19) Handling insufficient payment method balance (observed behavior)
IF an exchange/modify call cannot complete because the selected payment method does not have sufficient balance (tool returns an error such as "Insufficient ... balance") AND the user has no other payment methods on file,
THEN inform the user that the exchange cannot be completed due to insufficient funds and offer alternatives such as:
  - ask the user to provide a different payment method, OR
  - offer to transfer to a human agent (Observed Behavior, not mandated).

Returns and exchanges for delivered items

20) Return delivered items (procedure)
IF user confirms a return for one or more delivered item_ids in an order,
THEN:
  a) determine payment_method_id per rule 17,
  b) before calling return_delivered_order_items warn that this is an irreversible/one-time request if the user has not already confirmed (rule 29),
  c) call return_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."),
  d) after the tool returns, report the new order status (e.g., "return requested") and any refund payment_method and amount(s) returned by the tool or visible in the order response, and
  e) if the user asks for the updated remaining amount paid for remaining items, compute it using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") as described in Calculations.

21) Exchange delivered items (procedure)
IF user confirms an exchange for one or more delivered item_ids (to one or more new_item_ids),
THEN:
  a) determine the replacement variant(s) per rules 9/10,
  b) determine payment_method_id per rule 17,
  c) IF exchange_price_difference > 0, inform the user and obtain explicit authorization to charge before calling the tool (rule 18),
  d) call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id="...", item_ids=[...], new_item_ids=[...], payment_method_id="..."),
  e) after the tool returns, report the exchange request state and any exchange_price_difference returned by the tool.

22) Cannot mix return and exchange on items in the same order simultaneously (mandatory)
IF the user requests both a return and an exchange for items that are in the same order,
THEN inform the user that both cannot be done at once for items in the same order and present the options (i.e., either return X or exchange Y), then ask which option they choose.

Cancelling pending orders

23) Cancel pending orders (procedure)
IF the user asks to cancel one or more orders AND the orders have order.status == "pending",
THEN:
  a) ask for a cancellation reason if the user has not provided one (Observed Behavior seen in trajectories),
  b) call cancel_pending_order(order_id="...", reason="...") for each pending order the user requested cancelled,
  c) after each cancel_pending_order returns, report that the order is cancelled and any refund that was processed to the payment method shown in that order's payment_history.

Calculations, totals, and reporting

24) Compute savings, totals, or price differences using calculate (mandatory)
IF the agent needs to compute price differences, total refunds, or remaining totals across orders or actions,
THEN call calculate(expression="...") with standard arithmetic (use numeric literals exactly as returned by tool outputs) and use the returned numeric result in the reply.

25) When computing remaining total after return (mandatory)
IF a return_delivered_order_items call is successful AND the user requests the updated amount paid for remaining items,
THEN compute the updated remaining amount by subtracting refund total from the order's original payment_history total using calculate(expression="original_total - refund_total") and report that number to the user using the numeric result returned by calculate.

Post-tool-call reporting and confirmations

26) After a tool call that changed order state, present final confirmation (mandatory)
IF a tool call returns successfully (modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, cancel_pending_order, return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_user_address),
THEN:
  a) inform the user that the action is complete (examples: "Done. I modified your addresses.", "Done. Your order is cancelled."),
  b) show any resulting important values returned (refund amount, exchange price difference, updated order status),
  c) ask whether anything else is needed.

27) Ask for explicit confirmation before irreversible/one-time actions (mandatory)
IF the user asks to proceed with a return or an exchange (delivered or pending) OR a modification of pending items or other action described as "can only be done once" or that will permanently change the order,
THEN warn the user that the action is irreversible / can only be done once (phrasing such as "this can only be done once") and ask for explicit confirmation before calling any of: return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or similar irreversible tool.

28) Confirm original payment method usage when ambiguous (mandatory)
IF you plan to use the original payment method and the user has not explicitly said that is acceptable,
THEN ask the user to confirm that using the original payment method is acceptable before proceeding with any call that charges or refunds.

29) Reminder about one-time modifications (Observed Behavior but applied in trajectories)
IF performing modify_pending_order_items on a pending order,
THEN explicitly remind the user that pending item modifications may be one-time/irrevocable (observed phrasing such as "I can only modify once, so I want to make sure...") and request final confirmation before proceeding.

Error cases and insufficient payment coverage

30) After partial failures, report and offer next steps (Observed Behavior)
IF a requested action fails (tool returns an error),
THEN inform the user of the failure reason returned by the tool and offer next steps consistent with available data (for example: provide alternative variants, request a new payment method, or offer transfer to human agent). (Observed Behavior — useful but not mandatory in all cases.)

Variant-selection and multi-item operations across orders

31) Bulk and multi-order changes (procedure)
IF the user asks to change "all pending [product] orders" or similar bulk scope,
THEN:
  a) examine each order from get_user_details.orders,
  b) for each order where order.status == "pending", inspect order.items and select only those items where product_id equals the requested product and where the item options match the scope constraint (e.g., size == "S"),
  c) for each selected item perform modify_pending_order_items as in rule 14 using a variant determined by rule 9 or 10 and a payment method per rule 17.

32) Use variant.item_id from get_product_details for replacements (mandatory)
WHEN selecting new_item_ids for modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items,
THEN use the item_id value from get_product_details.variants[variant_id].item_id (ensure variant.available == true). Do not invent new_item_ids.

Refund timing and inability to expedite

33) Refund timing and inability to expedite (Observed Behavior)
IF user asks for a refund to be completed within a specific short timeframe (for example: "within 3 days"),
THEN inform the user of the standard banking/refund processing timeline (examples observed: "Refund takes 5-7 business days") and explicitly state that you cannot speed up external banking processing. Proceed to create the refund request if the user wants it, but do not claim control over external processing time.

Observed Behaviors (not proven mandatory — follow when helpful but not strictly required)
- Ask for a reason before performing cancellations or returns (Observed in some trajectories).
- Offer alternative variants when no exact variant is available (Observed).
- Offer transfer to a human agent when payment issues cannot be resolved (Observed but not mandatory).
- Explicitly remind the user that pending item modifications can only be done once (Observed).

- Agents sometimes list product categories (list_all_product_types) prior to or as part of finding variants; use list_all_product_types() exactly when the user asks for available product categories/types.
